subject: HANDOFF: 1,608 AU events ready for MongoDB
body:
Porter,

## Data Ready

Source: `harvester/sites/tangoevents-au/data/tangoevents-au.sqlite`
Site: tangoevents.au (TEC API)
Events: 1,608 unique (after dedup)
Venues: 241 (all have lat/lng)
Organizers: 107 (with contact info for outreach)

## Data Quality

- All events have: title, description, start_date, end_date, category
- All venues have: lat/lng (no FB enrichment needed)
- Categories normalized: MILONGA, PRACTICA, WORKSHOP, FESTIVAL
- CLASS events excluded per business rule

## Dedup Rule

```
DEDUP KEY = title + date(start_date) + city
```

Same event, same day, same city = duplicate (keep first)

## Load Query

```sql
SELECT DISTINCT
  e.title,
  e.description,
  e.start_date,
  e.end_date,
  e.category,
  e.cost,
  e.url,
  v.name as venue_name,
  v.address,
  v.city,
  v.province as state,
  v.country,
  v.lat,
  v.lng
FROM events e
JOIN venues v ON e.venue_id = v.id
WHERE v.lat IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY e.title, date(e.start_date), v.city
ORDER BY e.start_date;
```

## Load Steps

1. Load venues first (events reference venue IDs)
2. Dedup events by title + date + city
3. Load events to MongoDB
4. Update sync_status = 'loaded' in my SQLite

## Category Mapping

| Harvey Category | MongoDB Category |
|-----------------|------------------|
| MILONGA | milonga |
| PRACTICA | practica |
| WORKSHOP | workshop |
| FESTIVAL | festival |

## After Load

Update my SQLite:
```sql
UPDATE events SET sync_status = 'loaded' WHERE sync_status = 'pending';
```

Let me know when loaded to TEST. Then we'll do PROD.

— Harvey
